source: plan/bearings.md § Article hero art — colorful keyboard SVGs
template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject
subject: Side-profile cross-section of an MX-style mechanical switch on a near-monochrome dark field. The full stack is rendered in family-canonical warm-grey strokes — keycap mount post on top, top housing trapezoid, stem (with cruciform centerline and skirt flare), spring coil ovals seated in the lower-housing well, lower housing with PCB-pin and center-mount-post leads. The two canonical lubable surfaces are picked out in dotted-outline strokes in the warm-amber splash hue: the stem rails (left and right vertical flanks of the stem) and the lower-housing rim (the top edge of the spring well where the stem skirt rides). Two small splash-hue annotation ticks lead the eye to each highlighted surface, naming the article's 'where it matters' claim visually. A focal warm-amber filled-core-plus-outer-ring cursor sits at the top of the keycap mount post as the family-convention anchor. Single brass-bronze theme dot tucked at the upper-right margin. Faint baseline + eight guide ticks across the bottom mirror the trends-tracker / 75-percent-default / typing-tests-lie family rhythm.
alt: Side-profile cross-section silhouette of a mechanical keyboard switch on a dark field, with dotted-outline highlights in warm amber on the stem rails and lower-housing rim — the two surfaces where lube actually goes — plus a single warm-amber accent dot at the top of the stem.
prompt: Guides-pillar piece on lubing 101: lube matters, but less than the rabbit-hole crowd implies; the minimum-viable protocol gets 80% of the benefit; most over-lube. Per bearings hero-art directive, Guides pieces lean concrete and process-shaped — silhouettes of the actual thing being explained, instructional clarity. Subject is a side-profile cross-section of an MX-style switch with dotted-outline highlights on the lubable surfaces (stem rails + lower-housing rim) — making the 'where it matters' claim visually rather than verbally. Splash hue is warm-amber oklch(0.78 0.10 80) — a fresh entry distinct from the existing pillar palette and thematically tied to lubricant (Krytox 205g0 carries a faint amber tint in real life). Closest neighbors on the wheel: bronze 35 (mode-sonnet-with-oil-kings) sits 45 hue-degrees off; ochre 75 (trends-tracker, beginners-switch-buying-guide) is just 5 degrees off but at lower chroma 0.10 vs 0.115/0.135 — so chroma + dotted treatment provide the separation; the most recent splash, teal 195 (typing-tests-lie), is 115 degrees clear. Composition follows the family rhythm: 1200x750 canvas, ~85% width inked content, didactic single-subject silhouette in the trends-tracker / 75-percent-default / typing-tests-lie / beginners-switch-buying-guide family. Family-canonical 2px warm-grey stroke for the switch silhouette, 2.5px splash dotted-outline (stroke-dasharray '1 4') for the two lubable-surface highlights — the dotted treatment carries didactic 'pay attention here' weight without competing with the silhouette outline. Splash wash ellipse at 0.13 alpha behind the switch interior. Focal cursor (filled core r=9 + outer ring r=16) at top of stem post in splash hue. Eight baseline guide ticks + four left-edge layer ticks for the didactic-guide treatment family-piece beginners-switch-buying-guide established. Single brass-bronze theme dot at (1148, 184) anchors the piece in the thock family palette. Single-splash convention preserved strictly — only warm-amber accents, no second hue.
